headline: one canonical answer beats a pile of candidates
sub: dump, retrieve, return one. the last step is the whole game. on our doc-heavy repo: ~60% fewer tokens per agent run (first-party).

headline: passed ci. still the wrong merge.
sub: an agent rewrote a config two other agents still read from. the review gate held it before merge.

headline: faster code, shakier delivery
sub: 'ai adoption negatively impacts software delivery stability and throughput.' the bottleneck was never typing speed.

headline: it asked twice
sub: the agent re-derived context it had 20 minutes ago, same session. route it once instead: ~60% lighter per lookup (first-party). the round-trip tax is real.

headline: how do you keep agent context current?
sub: hand-edited markdown rots the second a file changes. asking, not telling.

headline: ai adoption up, delivery stability down
sub: the 2024 DORA report names the cause: larger changesets moving through the same gates.

headline: the cost moved, it didn't disappear
sub: duplication is cheap on the day you write it and expensive every day after.
source: tsukumo, does ai hurt code quality

headline: a passing demo answers nothing production asks
sub: unattended operation, plus a sign-off your seniors trust at 2am. tsukumo.ch/readiness
